behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff between PR-change detection and file-analysis orchestration (path mapping/filtering). A pure unit test of filter logic can miss wiring bugs where unchanged files are still queued. No real OS/network/subprocess is required for the core failure mode, and no AI output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run the lint step in-process with: (1) a fixture repo tree containing changed and unchanged files, (2) a deterministic changed-file provider returning only PR files, and (3) a stub linter executor. Assert the executor is invoked only for changed-file paths and never for unchanged paths.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: Real risk is in wiring parser/aggregator/formatter/publisher payload together; grouping can be correct in isolation but wrong in final posted body. Rejected System because behavior text is about grouping structure, not real network behavior or GitHub API edge conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute findings-to-comment pipeline with mixed-severity findings and a fake PR-comment client. Assert the final posted comment body contains distinct severity sections (error/warning/info) and each finding appears under the correct section.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a runtime/timeout SLO in GitHub Actions. Real elapsed time, process scheduling, filesystem IO, and subprocess execution determine pass/fail; mocks can hide timeout failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual lint-step entrypoint in a CI-like environment against a generated 200-file PR fixture using real file IO and real linter subprocess execution. Measure wall-clock duration and assert total completion is under 10 minutes, including result aggregation/posting path.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real subprocess crash semantics (non-zero exit, signal/termination, stderr behavior) and recovery in the runner. In-process fakes often cannot reproduce crash handling accurately; mocking may always pass.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real linter subprocess (or crash-test shim binary) configured to crash on one malformed fixture file while succeeding on others. Run the full step and assert: crashed file is reported as failed, subsequent files are still analyzed, and a final PR results comment is still emitted.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is deterministic dedup logic over finding records (same file/line/signature). No required module boundary, OS/network/subprocess, or AI dependency. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require proving cross-module orchestration, only correctness of dedup result.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the dedup function with overlapping-rule findings targeting identical file+line (and same canonical message/signature) plus near-duplicates on different lines/messages. Assert exact deduped set and stable precedence/merge behavior.
